Risk Description: Privileged account passwords stolen via phishing or credential stuffing
PAM Mitigation: MFA enforced at Bastion — stolen password alone insufficient

Risk Description: Generic accounts (admin / root) prevent individual accountability
PAM Mitigation: Credential injection from vault — sessions attributed to named user

Risk Description: Persistent vendor VPN accounts not reviewed or expired
PAM Mitigation: JIT access via Access Manager — time-limited sessions

Risk Description: No audit trail of privileged actions — forensics impossible post-incident
PAM Mitigation: Full session recording for all proxied connections

Risk Description: Former employees / contractors retain access after offboarding
PAM Mitigation: AD integration — disabling account removes all Bastion access immediately

Risk Description: Direct IT-to-OT access bypasses all controls
Current Controls: No Bastion in front of OT
PAM Mitigation: Bastion deployed in OT DMZ — sole ingress for all privileged OT sessions

Risk Description: Static credentials on target systems never rotated
Authorised user abuses privileged access with no monitoring
PAM Mitigation: Session recording + SIEM alerting + JIT approval workflow

Risk Description: No evidence of privileged access controls for audit (NIS2 / IEC 62443)
PAM Mitigation: Structured audit reports + session recording retention + compliance mapping

Risk Description: Single Bastion node — outage blocks all privileged access
PAM Mitigation: HA deployment — Active-Active or Active-Passive with automatic failover

Risk Description: Legacy PLCs not reachable via modern protocols — outside PAM scope
PAM Mitigation: Serial-to-IP converter + Telnet proxy through Bastion
